nodes.name: Validate Newsletter
nodes.notes: Ensures only validated newsletter drafts proceed to logging and delivery.

parameters.text:
=Extract the top forex exchange rates against the Kenyan Shilling (KES).

Return ONLY valid JSON.

source = "FloatRates"
title = "FloatRates Forex Rates"
base_currency = "KES"

Include major currencies such as USD, GBP, EUR, ZAR, JPY, AUD, CAD and any other available currencies.

For each currency return:
- currency
- kes_per_unit

=Extract the top forex exchange rates against the Kenyan Shilling (KES).

Return ONLY valid JSON.

source = "NCBA"
title = "NCBA Rates"
base_currency = "KES"

Include major currencies such as USD, GBP, EUR, ZAR, JPY, AUD, CAD and any other available currencies.

For each currency return:
- currency
- kes_per_unit

=Extract the forex exchange rates from NCBA data.

Return ONLY valid JSON.

source = "NSE"
title = "NSE Forex Rates"
base_currency = "KES"

Include major currencies such as USD, GBP, EUR, ZAR, JPY, AUD and CAD.

For each currency return:
- currency
- kes_per_unit

You are a financial data analyst.

Analyze the provided ExchangeRate API data and extract the major forex exchange rates against the Kenyan Shilling (KES).

Return ONLY valid JSON.

Requirements:
- source = "ExchangeRate API"
- title = "ExchangeRate API Forex Rates"
- base_currency = "KES"
- Include only major currencies: USD, GBP, EUR, ZAR, JPY, AUD, CAD.
- For each currency provide:
  - currency
  - kes_per_unit
- Do not include explanations.
- Do not include markdown.
- Do not include text outside the JSON.

Example format:

{
  "source": "ExchangeRate API",
  "title": "ExchangeRate API Forex Rates",
  "base_currency": "KES",
  "rates": [
    {
      "currency": "USD",
      "kes_per_unit": "132.50"
    }
  ]
}

=You are the Chief Editor.

Review the forex comparison dashboard and create a concise financial newsletter.

Include:
- Headline
- Brief summary of key exchange rate observations
- Mention any notable differences between sources

Keep the newsletter under 200 words.

Return valid JSON only.

=Generate a simplified newsletter intro using available summaries.

STRICT RULES:

1. Under 200 words
2. Plain text only
3. Use simple professional tone
4. Return ONLY valid JSON

Input summaries:
{{ JSON.stringify($json, null, 2) }}

Return ONLY valid JSON in this format:

{
"newsletter_intro": "string",
"word_count": number,
"model_used": "GPT-4o-mini",
"status": "fallback-success"
}

nodes.name: Validate FloatRates JSON1
nodes.notes: Prevents malformed AI outputs from entering downstream merge operations.

parameters.content:
SUCCESSFUL FOREX SOURCES

These sources are queried successfully and provide exchange rate data for comparison.

DATA EXTRACTION & VALIDATION

AI extracts forex rates into structured JSON.
Validation ensures only valid data reaches the dashboard.

MERGE STRATEGY

Only validated sources are merged.
Failed sources do not stop workflow execution.

REPORTING & DISTRIBUTION

Forex rates are merged into a comparison dashboard.
AI generates a newsletter which is emailed and archived.
